reviews.review_title: No server on this version either!!
reviews.review_description: Iv bought this first version after the 2nd version because the second had no server, I don't know why I thought the first one would be any better it's actually worse nothing I do on this game is being logged and iv paid £23 for it I think you should give me my money back or sort out the servers as people are still paying full price for something that's clearly not working anymore.

I gave it a five star because honestly I have nothing bad to say about it I just think that it’s really fun and that u guys should definitely get it ok bybye
I can only have fun for 10 minutes until the game crashes

The climb and the climb 2 (just get the bundle) are both amazing games. I have my fingers crossed for a climb 3 but sadly I don't think we will get one. I have played both for countless hours and they were two of the first games I bought years ago when quest 2 first launched. They are both five stars and very fun. It's a climbing simulation game that requires quick thinking, spacial awareness, and confidence.
I used to be deathly afraid of heights. When I got these games and first started playing my knees would buckle when I looked down. I would feel nauseous and get vertigo. Not from motion sickness but the crippling fear of heights I had dealt with since I was a kid. But I kept playing and in time I could look down. Eventually I wanted to look down. Because I could. I could do it and not feel fear. It was so empowering to face that fear because I'm not afraid of heights anymore. Irl I can go on rides and go places that I would have never done before when I was afraid of heights.
This game showed me that VR was more than games. They were experiences. They could enrich one's life as well as be recreational. So this game will always have a special place in my life. I recommend the climb series to anyone. They are done very well and are classic vr titles.

Due to hateful/abusive DMs I keep recieving from other users because of my reviews, im now editing my reviews to keep it short, 

This game is okay
Both games are so addictive. I've been playing the game for hours and am aiming for all achievements. Hopefully, it will be a Climb 3 in the future. Rope Swings? 👀

reviews.review_title: Was great, no longer works on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: I used to love this game and considered purchasing The Climb 2, but after re-installing The Climb and trying it again, none of the menus work and it won’t load past the menu screen for me. A pity, I would have enjoyed revisiting this one!

reviews.review_title: Feels more broken with each new stage
reviews.review_description:
The game started off feeling great, traversing slowly along the holds and being able to take your time was really fun. Unfortunately, the further you get in the game, the more apparent it is that none of the systems work very well. Once I moved on past the mellow walls onto the hard levels and boulder levels, I frequently noticed myself being launched off the wall at random times letting go of holds despite me still holding the grab button, and 
other game-breaking bugs. When the game allowed for slow methodical movement, it wasn’t as noticeable, however in the later, faster, higher impact stages of the game, it became extremely obvious that this game could barely function.

That being said, it was fun for a while in the beginning, and might be worth it if it’s on sale, hence the three stars.

Really hoping the sequel will patch out all these issues.

I first purchased The Climb 2 and love it, completed all stages and came again from the beginning. Then I thought if The Climb 1 was worth it or not, and the answer is YES!! If you enjoy part 2, part 1 is also great. The Climb series is a MUST HAVE experience for me. Come and get it now!!

This one crashes a lot on my Q3.

I find the climbing aspect therapeutic and the stamina management aspect kind of stressful. I like both aspects, but I think all routes should be open to tourist from the beginning so people can better enjoy the game in their own way.

Clearing debris from holds doesn’t seem to work as well in this one as the sequel. And having to half-hold while struggling with the physics isn’t fun.

Otherwise, I like these games a lot. It’s weird how visceral my reaction to sitting on a couch can be under the right circumstances. These games still give me sweaty palms.

This game is a lot of fun, however there are a few issues preventing this game from being 5 stars.
First of all, who's idea was it to make the screen go fully black if your head intersects with an object? Just make it so the head can't go through objects, so many games do that already, it's not hard. It's not annoying most of the time, but when you're hanging from a wire or a bit of hanging wood it's so annoying.
Second, some grips are just plain unfair. Not difficult to pull off, just unfair. Breakable grips that break instantly are just not fun, especially when you actually have to move off of them and they aren't just trick grips.
Other than that, this is a really good VR game and I'm excited to play more.
Awful in every way, and devs won't bother to fix it.

The good news, the app itself deserves 5+ Stars. The bad news is that the app appears  to have changed since I last purchased it a couple of years ago.

Specifically, I am a novice and I am not looking to compete with anyone but myself.  I just enjoy the thrill of climbing a challenging mountain. I am not into chalking up my hands and any other skill related tasks. 

With the old version of the game I was able to complete one climb and then go on to the next challenge as a "tourist". With this newer version, I cannot go on to the next level without jumping through hoops of mastery to get there. I just want to keep it simple and enjoy. 

If I am doing something wrong, please enlighten me and I will revise my review. As previously stated, the older oculus version allowed me to circumvent the "rules". For someone like myself, this version is worth about $1.99 as I only get to experience one climb and then it's "rinse and repeat".

It is really bad won’t a refund
￼

We are brand new to VR and this was one of our first games. The programming and amount of rendering that had to occur here is astounding. Hats off to the designers and programmers! Fun, realistic, good workout and intuitive. Love it and look forward to breaking through the levels.

reviews.review_title: DENIED A REFUND AFTER ONLY 20 MINS OF...
reviews.review_description:
Finally bought this game after months of debating, instantly became motion sick and it would only get worst the more I played.i spent no longer than 20 mins playing,  I knew this was not for me, I requested a refund only to be denied.

This is unacceptable for this game will now sit in my library unplayable.

This has now impacted my decision on purchasing any future games from the oculus store.

So the environment is really pretty and the game can make you sweat but i dont think you really get so much exercise from it. My only complaint is that when you get your head too close to an object it completely blacks out the screen and it can mess you up pretty bad in an intense situation. It seems like a pretty short game so idk how replayability is going to be but we'll see.

reviews.review_title: Game is beautiful but crashes. A LOT
reviews.review_description: Let me start with A LOT of positives; the game is beautiful, immersive and gives you an intense climbing experience. During climbs the adrenaline rushes through my body, the controls are quite realistic and time passes so fast during this game. The only thing is that there isn't much time per climb to enjoy everything about this game because it crashes so much. This happens during very different moments during the game. If the game updates to a more stable version this is a 5 star game.

There is great sense of achievement when you finally get to climb your first summit. My legs where somewhat shaking when looking down the feeling of being at high altitudes does feel real. What ever you do don’t look down.
Camera jumps with every hand motion. Crashed in the middle of tutorial. No idea how it got such high ratings

Going into this game i didn’t expect much i saw it in a youtube video thought it looked boring but said duck it lets give it a try and boy was it amazing. Looking down gives me an adrenaline rush and im not even scared of hights. Incredible game and a vr essential.
Can only finish 1 in 3 climbs without it crashing.  Would return if I could.

Why on the trailer to the game it says race friends and when you go in the game its single player.

Am i missing something?

Otherwise great game apart from that.

reviews.review_title: Fun but it crashes all the time
reviews.review_description: The game is fun but it keeps crashing in the middle of the level for randomly for no reason at all it's very frustrating.

The game is certainly not terrible in any way, in fact it’s one of my favorites! But it’s not very good for long play sessions, but it is a great way to calm down from a hard day and get a small workout!
If we’re being honest.
Graphics are amazing and the game is vary challenging but at time can be vary glitchy.
I expected a nice game, but its pretty immersive (except the far graphics). It feels really real and has some good mechanics. Got that Game its great.

It’s a really good game but I have one problem with it. It is a bit overpriced, some other people said it should be like $5 but I think it should be around 10-25. The fact that your head just goes into the wall and the game tells you to move it also is not the best but there isn’t much you can do besides that. The rest of the game is pretty fast but also really good. You can pick a lot of exciting paths and do them your own way (usually). A lot of people also say the graphics are trash but when you have a really small box to play real video and track controllers it can’t even see without external cameras or wires, it is pretty hard to make the graphics perfect. So I think they are pretty good.

reviews.review_title: Not as good as it seems
reviews.review_description: People say this game was Really good and the views are stunning. 1:The views are NOT GOOD they look like they are 8 bit and made horrible!  2:They dont really say much a lot about what the controls are about!   3:THIS GAME IS SO OVER PRICED!!!! I would say it would be about 7$ compared to 30$

This game is by far one of the most detailed games on the Oculus Quest. I very much enjoy this game but I was surprised to see it listed as one of the five most immersive games on the Quest. In games such as SuperHot and Beat Saber I lose myself in the complete immersion that these games offer. Unfortunately, I did not feel the same way about this game as I felt that I could just tell that I was in a game and was not lost in the immersion as I was in the other games. But saying that, I do love the game and what it has to offer. The tracking in this game is exception and I like how the game is simple in a sense but it is so hard to complete. As I just payed $400 for the Quest, I expected the games to be a bit cheaper than what the games are listed at and there is not exception for this game. I was not filled with joy when I saw this title listed at $30. But other than that, this game has so much to offer and it is a great overall experience. Another thing I will mention is that the duration of this game is great. In the Vader Immortal games, the only thing that I was dissapointed in is that I finished the whole series in around 3-4 hours. I also would not recommend this game to a beginner who just got the Oculus Quest as it requires a decent amount of skill. 

OVERALL = I LOVE “THE CLIMB”
I really enjoy this game. The breathtaking views are one thing but the actual game mechanics are fun and is a workout in itself.
This is the best game I have ever played on a VR
It’s fun

This is absolutely a five star game.

However it is very buggy. I’m using a new oculus quest 2 and I find the game crashes on average every hour of playtime. Please invest in making is more reliable on this platform, the platform is mainstream.

I think there’s a few quick wins they could roll out to significantly improve the user experience.

1. enable dark mode. The white background is really glaring, bad for your eyes, and not good to use late at night
